h1: Convert Employee Timesheet PDF to Excel for Payroll Processing

hero.headline: Transform Employee Timesheet PDFs into Excel Spreadsheets
hero.subheadline: Streamline payroll processing by converting paper timesheets and PDF forms into structured Excel data with AI-powered extraction

summary: Converting employee timesheets from PDF format to Excel eliminates manual data entry for payroll processing. This guide covers methods for extracting timesheet data including employee hours, dates, project codes, and overtime calculations into structured spreadsheets for payroll systems and labor cost analysis.

Payroll administrators handling multiple timesheet formats

AI identifies employee names, dates, hours worked, and overtime data
Review extracted fields and customize column headers for your payroll system

whyPdfExcelAiFits: OCR technology reads both typed and handwritten timesheet entries

limitations: Handwritten timesheet entries may require manual verification compared to typed forms

faq.question: Can I convert handwritten employee timesheets to Excel?
faq.answer: Yes, the OCR technology can process handwritten timesheets, though accuracy is higher on typed or printed timesheet forms. Handwritten entries may require manual review for verification.

faq.question: How do I handle different timesheet formats from multiple departments?
faq.answer: You can customize field extraction for each timesheet format and create separate processing workflows. The system learns to identify common fields like employee ID, hours, and dates across different layouts.

faq.question: Will overtime hours be calculated automatically in the Excel output?
faq.answer: The system extracts the raw timesheet data as entered. You'll need to set up Excel formulas or import the data into your payroll system for overtime calculations based on your company policies.

faq.question: Can I process an entire week's worth of employee timesheets at once?
faq.answer: Yes, batch processing allows you to upload multiple timesheet PDFs simultaneously. Each timesheet becomes a separate row in your Excel output with consistent column formatting.
